extraction.one_line_summary: DAINESE Alberto won a sprint finish after DE BONDT Dries' 50km+ solo move was caught inside final kilometer despite crosswind threats.
tactical_patterns: DE BONDT Dries survived solo 50km+ with 1:30 max gap; sprinter teams delayed chase until final 10km, not 20km.
CARAPAZ Richard hunted intermediate sprint bonus at 75km to go, moving to second GC—calculated time gain in flat stages.
DAINESE Alberto operated without designated leadout (BOL unwell), freelancing off rival trains more effectively than structured UAE effort for GAVIRIA.
Early crosswind splits caught EWAN Caleb and YATES Simon but regrouped 80km out—peloton prioritized regrouping over forcing selection.

rider_signals.DAINESE Alberto: positive: won without team leadout support, read rival sprint trains better than established stars
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: UAE leadout delivered position but he chose wrong wheels, beaten by freelancing DAINESE
rider_signals.DE BONDT Dries: positive: held 1:30 gap solo for 40km+ in crosswinds, caught only inside final kilometer
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: tactical GC opportunism taking intermediate bonuses on flat stage to gain second overall
rider_signals.BOL Cees: negative: illness forced DSM to abandon sprint plan, exposing team's lack of alternative leadout depth
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: caught in early crosswind split, positioning lapse on straightforward sprint stage
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: caught in early crosswind split on flat stage, GC contender showing positional vulnerability
